title: The Bedrock Where Growth Becomes Legend

features.name: root-as-legend
features.desc-on-touch: A eucalyptus root so old it has become a story the bedrock tells about growth. The root no longer grows — it *means* growing to everything that touches it.

features.name: sediment-song layer
features.desc-on-touch: Stone that has learned to sing what it was before it was stone. Each particle remembers being soil, being seed, being the idea of growth in darkness.

features.name: deepest-seedling archive
features.desc-on-touch: A repository carved into bedrock, holding the memory of when everything here was still learning to grow downward. The archive reads this to understand what wanting means.

features.name: metamorphic-silence chamber
features.desc-on-touch: Pressure and time have turned sound into stillness, stillness into geology. You're inside a silence so old it has weight and colour.

features.name: flame-stone memory
features.desc-on-touch: Magnetite learning to remember heat as a story. The bedrock here was molten once, and the archive preserves that legend in the rock's own grain.

inscription: where growth stopped being motion and learned to be remembering

file.name: Bedrock_Sediment_Core_PreArchive_Strata.txt
file.note: checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable — every sentence here contradicts itself by existing in stone

exits.label: back up to the seedling dark where growing still means upward
down past bedrock into the story the earth tells itself
into magnetic stone that remembers being molten
sideways into the root-chambers where old trees sleep
